dimensions.boardOrientation: Light square on right corner (h1 = light)

courtFieldImage.surfaceTypes: Cardboard, vinyl, wood, or silicone board
courtFieldImage.venues: Indoor: home, chess club, tournament hall

healthBenefits.benefit: Improves memory and concentration
Develops logical thinking and problem-solving skills
Reduces risk of dementia and Alzheimer's
Improves patience and emotional control
Reduces anxiety through structured thinking

warmup.stretching: Neck rotations, shoulder shrugs, wrist circles (5 min)
warmup.pregameExercises: Solve 5 simple tactics puzzles, review a favourite opening line

cooldown.postgameRecovery: Review the game briefly, stand up and walk, deep breathing, eye relaxation

basicRules.rule: Game is played between two players, one controlling white pieces, the other black.
White moves first. Players alternate moves.
Objective: checkmate the opponent's king.
Special moves: castling, en passant, pawn promotion.
Draw by stalemate, threefold repetition, or mutual agreement.

breaksIntervals.standard: No scheduled breaks in standard play
breaksIntervals.tournament: 30-min break may be scheduled after 2-3 hours

rulesRegulations.howToWin: Deliver checkmate, or opponent resigns, or opponent runs out of time

foulsAndPenalties.penalty: Illegal move: move retracted; in blitz: loss of game
Touch-move rule: if you touch a piece you must move it
Clock violation: loss of game if time runs out

commonInjuries.injury: Neck and back pain from prolonged sitting

preventionMethods.method: Use 20-20-20 rule: every 20 min, look 20 feet away for 20 seconds
Take a 2-minute break after every 30 min of play
Do neck stretches between games

diseasesToAvoidPlaying.condition: Migraine or severe headache
Recent concussion - cognitive rest needed

hydrationImportance.signsOfDehydration: Dry mouth, headache, irritability, reduced concentration

nutritionTips.preGame: Complex carbs + protein + healthy fat 1-2 hours before; avoid heavy meals
nutritionTips.duringGame: Water or unsweetened tea; small snacks like nuts in long games
nutritionTips.postGame: Protein + carbs to replenish mental energy
nutritionTips.daily: Omega-3 (fish, walnuts), antioxidants (blueberries), B vitamins for brain health

physicalBurnout.limit: Play serious games max 3-4 days per week
physicalBurnout.restDays: At least 2 days with no chess or only light puzzles
physicalBurnout.crossTrain: Physical exercise (walking, swimming) to clear mind

mentalBurnout.signs: Dreading study, making uncharacteristic blunders, loss of enjoyment
mentalBurnout.solution: Take 1-2 weeks break, play casual variants, reduce rating pressure

equipment.item: Chess pieces (plastic or wood, standard tournament size)
Score sheet and pen (for classical games)

safetyGear.item: Anti-glare glasses if sensitive to lighting
Wrist brace if prone to repetitive strain

coordination.desc: Mental coordination: integrate calculation, memory, and pattern recognition
coordination.drill: Solve tactical puzzles daily - 15 min on Chess.com or Lichess

speedAgility.desc: Mental speed: quickly recognise threats and candidate moves

focusReaction.desc: Maintain concentration for hours, avoid distractions

strategy.opening: Control centre, develop pieces, castle early
strategy.middlegame: Create weaknesses, improve piece activity, launch attack

xFactors.gameSense: Evaluate when to trade pieces, sacrifice, or simplify
xFactors.decisionMaking: Choose between candidate moves based on safety, activity, and long-term plan

dailyPlan.day: Tactics (20 puzzles) + Openings (15 min) + One rapid game
Endgame study + Analysis of master game
Advanced tactics + Review personal games from week
Rest or light puzzles only + physical activity

drillsForBeginners.drill: Mate with king+queen vs king (practice 10 times)
Place knight to fork two pieces - 10 positions
Play 5 games focusing only on centre control and development

focusingTechniques.technique: Inhale 4 sec, hold 2 sec, exhale 6 sec before critical moves
Imagine board and move sequences 3-4 moves deep

pressureHandling.tip: When low on time, play safe moves and rely on intuition
Use positive self-talk: I have a plan, I can execute it
After a blunder, look for counterplay or swindles

sportsmanship.rule: Resign only when defeat is certain
Don't distract opponent with noise or gestures
Congratulate opponent after loss

howToJoinSport.step: Learn rules and basic tactics free on Chess.com or Lichess
Buy a basic chess set or use free online platforms
Join a local chess club
Play rated games online to get a starting rating
Enter your first local tournament
howToJoinSport.costEstimate: Online free; club membership $20-50/year; tournament entry $20-50

strategies.strategy: Control centre, develop knights before bishops, castle quickly
Pawn chains, positional manoeuvring, slow build-up
Open files, sacrifice pieces, bring queen and rooks
Bring king to centre, use as attacking piece

terminologies.term: Check - king is under attack
Checkmate - king under attack with no legal escape
Stalemate - no legal moves but king not in check (draw)
Fork - single piece attacks two or more enemy pieces
Pin - piece cannot move without exposing a more valuable piece
Sacrifice - give up material for positional or attacking advantage
Zugzwang - any move worsens player's position
Blunder - very bad move that loses material or the game
En passant - special pawn capture
Castling - king and rook move together
Promotion - pawn reaches last rank and becomes a stronger piece
Elo rating - numerical measure of player strength
Gambit - opening where a pawn is sacrificed for development
FIDE - International Chess Federation

courtFieldImage.surfaceTypes: Indoor heated pool, outdoor lido, open water (lake, sea, river)
courtFieldImage.venues: Aquatic centres, leisure centres, open water events

healthBenefits.benefit: Full-body cardiovascular workout with low joint impact
Builds muscle strength and endurance across all major groups
Improves lung capacity and respiratory efficiency
Reduces stress and anxiety (meditative rhythm)
Excellent rehabilitation exercise for injuries
Burns 400-700 calories per hour

warmup.stretching: Arm circles, shoulder rotations, hip flexor stretch, ankle rolls (5 min)
warmup.pregameExercises: 200 m easy freestyle warm-up swim at 50% effort

cooldown.postgameRecovery: 200 m easy backstroke cool-down, static stretching of shoulders and calves poolside

basicRules.rule: Swimmers must stay in their assigned lane throughout the race.
False start results in disqualification (one false start allowed in some formats).
Freestyle: any stroke allowed; most use front crawl.
Backstroke: must touch wall on back; back at all times except turns.
Breaststroke: simultaneous arm pull and frog kick; touch wall with both hands.
Butterfly: simultaneous arm recovery over water; dolphin kick only.

breaksIntervals.standard: Rest between heats: 30 min to 2 hours depending on event

scoringSystem.win: Fastest time wins; points awarded by place in multi-event meets

rulesRegulations.howToWin: Touch the wall first in your lane with correct stroke technique

foulsAndPenalties.penalty: Disqualification (DQ) for false start, stroke violation, or missing touch
Lane infringement: DQ if interfering with another swimmer

preventionMethods.method: Warm up thoroughly before entering the pool
Rotate training between all four strokes to prevent overuse
Use earplugs to prevent swimmer's ear
Maintain correct technique - poor form causes most injuries
Strengthen rotator cuff with resistance band exercises on land

diseasesToAvoidPlaying.condition: Ear infection - worsened by water exposure
Open wounds or skin infections - risk of contamination
Severe asthma without medical clearance

hydrationImportance.beforeMatch: 500 ml water 2 hours before; 250 ml 30 min before
hydrationImportance.afterMatch: 500-750 ml water or electrolyte drink immediately after
hydrationImportance.warningAvoid: Do not rely on thirst - you sweat in water but don't feel it
hydrationImportance.signsOfDehydration: Dizziness, cramps, fatigue, dark urine post-swim

nutritionTips.preGame: Light meal 2-3 hours before: pasta, rice, or bread with protein (chicken, tuna)
nutritionTips.duringGame: Energy gels or banana between heats for longer sessions
nutritionTips.postGame: Recovery shake or chocolate milk within 30 min; full meal within 2 hours
nutritionTips.daily: High protein diet (1.4-1.7 g/kg body weight); complex carbs for energy

physicalBurnout.limit: Max 10-12 sessions per week for elite; 4-6 for recreational
physicalBurnout.restDays: One full rest day per week minimum
physicalBurnout.crossTrain: Dryland training (gym, yoga) to balance pool work

mentalBurnout.signs: Dreading pool, slower times despite more effort, persistent fatigue
mentalBurnout.solution: Reduce volume, swim for fun, change training environment

equipment.item: Swimsuit (competitive: tech suit; training: polyester or chlorine-resistant)

safetyGear.item: Swim cap - reduces drag and protects hair

coordination.desc: Synchronising arm pull, kick, and breathing rhythm for each stroke
coordination.drill: Catch-up drill: one arm stays extended until other hand passes it

speedAgility.desc: Explosive start off blocks and fast flip turns

focusReaction.desc: React instantly to starting signal; maintain focus over long distances

strategy.offensive: Negative split strategy - swim second half faster than first
strategy.defensive: Pace control in distance events to avoid early burnout

xFactors.gameSense: Reading competition and knowing when to surge
xFactors.decisionMaking: Choosing stroke rate vs distance-per-stroke balance

dailyPlan.day: Sprint sets (10 x 50 m) + dryland strength

drillsForBeginners.drill: Hold kickboard, kick freestyle - develop leg strength
Alternate one arm at a time to focus on pull technique
Practice bilateral breathing every 3 strokes

focusingTechniques.technique: Mentally swim the entire race the night before
Repeat a cue word (e.g., "smooth", "strong") per stroke cycle

pressureHandling.tip: Focus on process (stroke mechanics) not outcome (place)
Use pre-race routine (same warm-up, same music) to calm nerves
Control breathing on the blocks before the start signal

sportsmanship.rule: Stay in your lane and don't interfere with other swimmers
Congratulate competitors after race regardless of result

howToJoinSport.step: Find a local pool offering adult or youth swimming lessons
Learn the four competitive strokes with a qualified coach
Join a swimming club affiliated with national federation
Register for your first graded time trial or club gala

qualitiesOfGoodPlayer.quality: Mental toughness in pain threshold events

strategies.strategy: Swim the second half of the race faster than the first
Maintain consistent speed throughout (best for distance)
Go out fast, build a lead, hold on
Stay close behind a faster swimmer to reduce drag (open water)

terminologies.term: Stroke - one complete arm cycle
Flip turn - tumble turn at wall for freestyle and backstroke
Touch turn - open turn used in breaststroke and butterfly
Drag suit - extra resistance suit worn in training
DPS - distance per stroke
Pace clock - poolside clock showing elapsed time
Heat - preliminary round of a swimming race
PB / PR - personal best / personal record
FINA / World Aquatics - international governing body
Taper - reducing training load before a major competition
Split - time for a portion of the race (e.g., 50 m split in a 200 m race)

courtFieldImage.surfaceTypes: Dark green or blue painted wood or fibreglass table; net height 15.25 cm
courtFieldImage.venues: Sports halls, clubs, community centres, basements

healthBenefits.benefit: Improves hand-eye coordination and reflexes dramatically
Burns 270-400 calories per hour
Enhances cognitive function and tactical thinking
Low-impact aerobic exercise suitable for all ages
Improves balance and core stability

warmup.stretching: Wrist rotations, shoulder circles, light jog or footwork shuffle (5 min)
warmup.pregameExercises: 10 min rally with partner focusing on consistency; multiball warm-up

cooldown.postgameRecovery: 5 min easy rallying, forearm and wrist stretches, light shoulder stretch

basicRules.rule: Games are played to 11 points; win by 2 clear points.
Best of 5 or 7 games in most competitions.
Serve must be behind the end line, ball tossed 16 cm, struck to bounce on server's side then opponent's.
Service alternates every 2 points (except deuce at 10-10, then every point).
Ball must clear the net and land on opponent's half; edge of table counts.
A "let" is called if the ball clips the net on service; re-serve.

breaksIntervals.standard: 1-2 min break between games; towel break every 6 points in some events

scoringSystem.win: Win a game by reaching 11 points first (by 2 clear)

rulesRegulations.howToWin: Win the majority of games in the match (best of 5 or 7)

foulsAndPenalties.penalty: Point lost for illegal serve (not tossing ball 16 cm, hiding contact)
Point lost for hitting ball before it bounces on your side
Obstruction: touching table or moving it results in point loss

commonInjuries.injury: Elbow tendinitis (tennis elbow from topspin strokes)

preventionMethods.method: Warm up wrists and forearms thoroughly before play
Use correct grip to reduce elbow stress (shakehand vs penhold)
Strengthen core to protect lower back during rotation
Wear quality non-slip shoes for fast footwork

diseasesToAvoidPlaying.condition: Tennis elbow flare-up - rest and ice before returning
Wrist fracture or sprain - requires healing time

hydrationImportance.duringMatch: Sip between games; 150 ml every 15-20 min of play
hydrationImportance.afterMatch: 400 ml water or sports drink after session
hydrationImportance.warningAvoid: Sugary drinks cause energy spikes and crashes mid-match
hydrationImportance.signsOfDehydration: Muscle cramps, slowed reaction, loss of focus

nutritionTips.preGame: Light snack 1 hour before: banana, toast with nut butter
nutritionTips.duringGame: Water; glucose tablets or small fruit if match runs long
nutritionTips.postGame: Protein + carbs: yogurt with fruit, or chicken and rice
nutritionTips.daily: Balanced diet with lean protein, complex carbs, and vegetables

physicalBurnout.limit: Max 2-hour sessions; avoid daily high-intensity sessions
physicalBurnout.crossTrain: Footwork ladder drills, running, and core exercises off-table

mentalBurnout.signs: Loss of motivation, frustration at plateau, skipping practice
mentalBurnout.solution: Play social games, try doubles, take a week off

equipment.item: Table tennis bat/paddle (ITTF approved rubber on both sides)
Table tennis balls (3-star ITTF approved, 40 mm plastic)
Non-marking athletic shoes with good lateral support
Comfortable sportswear allowing free arm movement

safetyGear.item: Proper footwear - prevents ankle rolls on fast lateral movement
Wrist brace - optional for players with existing wrist issues

coordination.desc: Hand-eye coordination to read spin and place ball precisely
coordination.drill: Multi-ball training: coach feeds 100 balls rapidly to one spot

speedAgility.desc: Explosive footwork to get in position for every shot

focusReaction.desc: React to ball in under 0.3 seconds at elite level

strategy.offensive: Aggressive topspin loop to force weak returns
strategy.defensive: Chop defense - heavy backspin to neutralise attacks

xFactors.gameSense: Read opponent's serve and anticipate placement
xFactors.decisionMaking: Know when to attack vs play safe

dailyPlan.day: Footwork drills + forehand loop practice (1 hr)

drillsForBeginners.drill: Hit 50 consecutive forehands cross-court with partner
Bounce ball on bat 50 times each side without dropping
Practice basic pendulum serve 20 times per session

focusingTechniques.technique: Take a breath and reset between every point
Same physical routine before every serve to stay calm

pressureHandling.tip: At deuce, focus on serve placement not outcome
Slow down between points when nervous to regain control

sportsmanship.rule: Acknowledge good shots by opponent
Don't dispute edges - call your own faults honestly

howToJoinSport.step: Find a local table tennis club or leisure centre with tables
Buy a beginner bat ($20-40) and practice basics
Join a club and enter league play
Register with national association for graded tournaments

strategies.strategy: Heavy topspin to force opponent into defensive position
Mix of fast, short, and heavy spin serves to disrupt rhythm
Attack short ball, follow up with smash
Heavy backspin returns to force errors from aggressive opponent

terminologies.term: Topspin - forward spin making ball dip and kick up
Backspin - reverse spin making ball skid low
Loop - heavy topspin stroke brushed upward
Smash - flat fast attacking shot
Chop - defensive stroke with heavy backspin
Let - serve replayed when ball clips the net
Deuce - score tied at 10-10
ITTF - International Table Tennis Federation
Penhold grip - Chinese/Japanese style grip
Shakehand grip - most common European grip

dimensions.netHeight: 1.55 m at sides / 1.524 m at centre

courtFieldImage.surfaceTypes: Wooden sprung floor, synthetic mat, or outdoor concrete/asphalt
courtFieldImage.venues: Sports halls, recreation centres, outdoor courts

healthBenefits.benefit: One of the best calorie-burning sports (450-550 cal/hr)
Improves agility, reflexes, and hand-eye coordination
Excellent cardiovascular workout
Strengthens core, legs, and upper body
Improves mental alertness and concentration

warmup.stretching: Lunges, hamstring stretch, shoulder rotations, wrist and ankle circles (5 min)
warmup.pregameExercises: Footwork shadow practice, easy net shots and clears (10 min)

cooldown.postgameRecovery: Easy walking, static stretches for quads, calves, and shoulders

basicRules.rule: Games are played to 21 points; must win by 2 clear points (cap at 30-29).
Match is best of 3 games.
Rally scoring: a point is scored on every rally regardless of who served.
Service must be below the server's waist; shuttlecock hit diagonally to opposite service box.
Shuttlecock landing on the line is IN.
Players switch ends at start of third game and when first player reaches 11.

matchTimings.match: Approx 40-90 minutes for a full match

breaksIntervals.standard: 90-second break at 11 points in each game; 2-minute break between games

scoringSystem.win: First to 21 points (by 2); at 20-20, first to lead by 2; cap at 30-29

foulsAndPenalties.penalty: Fault: illegal serve, hitting shuttle out, shuttle touching body
Let: shuttle caught in net, external disturbance - rally replayed
Service fault: racket above waist, feet off ground on serve

commonInjuries.injury: Ankle sprain (most common - landing from jumps)

preventionMethods.method: Always wear proper non-slip badminton shoes
Strengthen ankles with balance exercises
Land softly from jumps with bent knees
Don't over-grip the racket
Strengthen shoulder rotator cuff with resistance bands

diseasesToAvoidPlaying.condition: Ankle sprain - needs full healing before return
Knee tendinitis flare-up - rest and ice

hydrationImportance.duringMatch: 150-200 ml every 15-20 minutes; use breaks between games
hydrationImportance.afterMatch: 500 ml water or electrolyte drink after match
hydrationImportance.warningAvoid: Avoid energy drinks - cause irregular heartbeat during high-intensity play
hydrationImportance.signsOfDehydration: Muscle cramps (common in calves), fatigue, reduced reaction time

nutritionTips.preGame: Pasta or rice 2-3 hours before; banana 30 min before for quick energy
nutritionTips.duringGame: Water; isotonic sports drink between games for matches over 1 hour
nutritionTips.postGame: Protein shake or chicken sandwich within 30 min for recovery
nutritionTips.daily: Carbohydrate-rich diet; lean protein; omega-3 for joint health

physicalBurnout.limit: No more than 2-hour sessions; 5-6 sessions per week maximum
physicalBurnout.crossTrain: Running for stamina, gym for strength, yoga for flexibility

mentalBurnout.signs: Frustration on court, dreading matches, persistent fatigue
mentalBurnout.solution: Play social doubles, take a week off, set small achievable goals

equipment.item: Badminton racket (lightweight: 80-100 g; graphite for competitive)
Shuttlecock (feather for competition; nylon for recreational)
Grip tape or overgrip for sweat absorption

safetyGear.item: Knee brace (for players with knee issues)

coordination.desc: Hand-eye coordination for precise shuttle placement and reading opponent's shot
coordination.drill: Solo feeding practice against wall; toss shuttle and hit at specific target

speedAgility.desc: Explosive footwork to cover all six corners of the court

focusReaction.desc: React to shuttle speed (can exceed 400 km/h in smash)

strategy.offensive: Powerful jump smash to back corners
strategy.defensive: Tight net play and lift to force shuttle above opponent's head

xFactors.gameSense: Move opponent around court to create space and fatigue them
xFactors.decisionMaking: Choose drop shot vs smash vs clear based on shuttle height

dailyPlan.day: Smash and defence (1 hr) + fitness run
Serve and return practice (30 min) + clears and drops

drillsForBeginners.drill: Hit full-length clears from baseline to baseline 20 times
Practice soft net drops from mid-court 15 times each side
Shadow all 6 corners without shuttle, 3 sets of 1 minute

focusingTechniques.technique: Bounce on toes, tap racket, take breath before each point
Pick specific court zone to aim for on each shot

pressureHandling.tip: At 20-20, focus on serve placement and first-shot attack
Slow down service motion when nervous to increase accuracy

sportsmanship.rule: Acknowledge opponent's good shots
Don't argue line calls; play a let if genuinely unsure

howToJoinSport.step: Find a local badminton club or leisure centre court
Buy a beginner racket ($20-50) and feather or nylon shuttles
Take beginner group coaching sessions
Enter local club ladder or league matches

strategies.strategy: Both players attack from rear and mid-court alternately
Side-by-side when under attack; one covers net, one covers rear
Jump smash, partner covers net for kill shot
Keep opponent pinned at back with deep clears

terminologies.term: Shuttle / shuttlecock - the feathered or plastic projectile
Smash - powerful downward attacking shot
Clear - high deep shot to opponent's baseline
Drop - soft shot falling close to net
Drive - flat fast shot played horizontally
Net kill - fast put-away shot from above net tape
Let - rally replayed due to interference or disturbance
Fault - rule violation costing a point
BWF - Badminton World Federation (governing body)
Love - zero (score of zero)

healthBenefits.benefit: Burns 600-900 calories per hour
Builds explosive leg power and upper body strength
Improves coordination, agility, and spatial awareness
Develops teamwork and communication skills
Cardiovascular endurance from continuous running

warmup.stretching: Dynamic leg swings, hip circles, shoulder arm swings, calf raises (5 min)
warmup.pregameExercises: Layup lines, free throw shooting, defensive slides (10 min)

cooldown.postgameRecovery: Light shooting, static stretching of quads, hamstrings, and hip flexors

basicRules.rule: 5 players per side on court; unlimited substitutions.
Ball advanced by dribbling; no carrying or double-dribble.
Field goal: 2 points inside arc, 3 points outside arc, 1 point free throw.
Shot clock: 24 seconds (NBA) / 30 seconds (FIBA) to attempt a shot.
Backcourt violation: cannot return ball to own half once past half-court.
Player fouls out after 5 fouls (FIBA) or 6 fouls (NBA).

breaksIntervals.standard: 2-minute break between quarters; 15-minute halftime; timeouts 30 sec or 60 sec

scoringSystem.win: Team with most points at end of regulation or overtime wins

rulesRegulations.howToWin: Score more points than the opponent

foulsAndPenalties.penalty: Personal foul: contact on shooter gives 2 or 3 free throws
Technical foul: unsportsmanlike conduct gives opponent 1 free throw + possession
Flagrant foul: severe contact - ejection possible

commonInjuries.injury: Shin splints from running on hard floors

preventionMethods.method: Wear ankle braces or tape ankles preventively
Land with bent knees to absorb impact
Strengthen VMO (quad muscle) for knee stability
Wear proper basketball shoes with ankle support
Use mouthguard to protect teeth in physical play

diseasesToAvoidPlaying.condition: Heart conditions - intense sprinting can trigger cardiac events
Concussion - must pass return-to-play protocol

hydrationImportance.beforeMatch: 500 ml water 2 hours before; 250 ml 30 min before
hydrationImportance.duringMatch: 150-200 ml every timeout or quarter break
hydrationImportance.afterMatch: 750 ml water or electrolyte drink; continue drinking throughout recovery
hydrationImportance.warningAvoid: Avoid carbonated drinks before play - causes bloating and cramps
hydrationImportance.signsOfDehydration: Cramps, dizziness, reduced jump height, loss of concentration

nutritionTips.preGame: Pasta or rice with chicken 3 hours before; banana or energy bar 1 hour before
nutritionTips.duringGame: Orange slices or banana at halftime; sports drink during timeouts
nutritionTips.postGame: Recovery meal within 30 min: protein shake, then full meal within 2 hours
nutritionTips.daily: High carbohydrate and protein diet; 3-5 g carbs per kg body weight on game days

physicalBurnout.limit: Max 90-min practice sessions; 5-6 days per week
physicalBurnout.crossTrain: Swimming, cycling, or yoga for active recovery

mentalBurnout.signs: Loss of motivation, poor on-court communication, declining enjoyment
mentalBurnout.solution: Set individual and team goals, vary training drills, watch inspiring games

equipment.item: Basketball (size 7 men / size 6 women / size 5 youth - FIBA standard)

safetyGear.item: Knee pads (for players prone to floor burns)

coordination.desc: Dribbling while scanning court, passing accurately under pressure
coordination.drill: Dribble two balls simultaneously for 2 minutes without looking at hands

speedAgility.desc: First-step explosiveness, lateral quickness, and sprint endurance

focusReaction.desc: Read defender movements, anticipate passing lanes, react to shot clock

strategy.offensive: Pick and roll, drive and kick, transition fast break
strategy.defensive: Switching defence, zone trap, help-side positioning

xFactors.gameSense: Know when to attack, when to pass, when to hold the ball
xFactors.decisionMaking: Choose shoot, pass, or drive in under 1 second

dailyPlan.day: Ball handling drills + shooting (1 hr) + fitness
Film study + free throw practice (30 min)
Pick and roll plays + transition drills

drillsForBeginners.drill: Alternating layups under the basket (left and right)
Dribble around chairs in zigzag pattern both hands
Shoot 10 shots from 5 spots around the key

focusingTechniques.technique: Same bounce-routine-shot sequence every free throw
Play one possession at a time; forget previous mistakes

pressureHandling.tip: In clutch moments, slow down and trust your training
Communicate with teammates to reduce individual pressure

sportsmanship.rule: Help opponents up after falls
Accept referee decisions without argument
Celebrate team success, not individual glory

howToJoinSport.step: Find a local YMCA, school, or recreational league
Buy a good basketball and practice fundamentals daily
Join open gym sessions to meet players and teams
Register for a local recreational league or tryout for school team

qualitiesOfGoodPlayer.quality: Basketball IQ - understanding of the game

strategies.strategy: Screen for ball handler, roll to basket for pass
Players guard areas not individual opponents
Push ball up court quickly before defense can set
Spacing and movement to create open shots

terminologies.term: Dribble - bounce ball with one hand while moving
Layup - shot close to basket off the backboard
Three-pointer - shot scored from beyond the arc
Pick and roll - screen play for ball handler
Fast break - quick offensive push after turnover
Turnover - losing possession without a shot
Rebound - recovering ball after missed shot
Assist - pass leading directly to a score
Foul - illegal contact with opponent
Free throw - uncontested shot from foul line
FIBA - International Basketball Federation
NBA - National Basketball Association

dimensions.balanceBeamHeight: 125 cm above floor; 5 m long, 10 cm wide

courtFieldImage.surfaceTypes: Spring floor, foam mats, padded landing areas, leather-covered apparatus

healthBenefits.benefit: Exceptional body strength relative to body weight
Extraordinary flexibility and range of motion
Superior body coordination and spatial awareness
Builds discipline and mental toughness from early age
Improves posture and core stability

warmup.stretching: Full-body dynamic stretch: leg swings, trunk rotations, wrist/shoulder circles (10 min)
warmup.pregameExercises: Cartwheel and handstand practise, run-through of floor routine at 50% intensity

cooldown.postgameRecovery: Full-body passive stretching: splits, shoulder stretch, back arch; foam rolling

basicRules.rule: Athletes scored by a panel of judges on Difficulty (D) and Execution (E) scores.
Final score = D score + E score (max E is 10.0; deductions for errors).
Deductions: 0.1 (small error), 0.3 (medium), 0.5 (fall from apparatus), 1.0 (fall to floor).
Time limits apply to floor exercise routines (70-90 seconds for women).
Stepping out of bounds on floor costs 0.1 per foot outside line.

rulesRegulations.numberOfPlayers: Individual or team of 5 (3 scores count)

matchTimings.routine: Each event routine 30-90 seconds (depends on apparatus)

breaksIntervals.standard: Rest between rotations: 15-30 min while other athletes compete

scoringSystem.win: Highest combined D + E score wins; team totals summed across events

rulesRegulations.howToWin: Score the highest combined total across all apparatus

foulsAndPenalties.penalty: Fall from apparatus: 1.0 deduction
Boundary violation: 0.1 deduction per foot
Time violation: 0.1-0.3 deduction

commonInjuries.injury: Wrist sprain and growth plate injuries (young gymnasts)

preventionMethods.method: Always use crash mats and spotters for new skills
Progress skills gradually - master lower difficulty before advancing
Strengthen wrists with specific conditioning exercises
Use proper landing technique: absorb with bent knees, soft feet
Take growth plate pain seriously in young athletes

diseasesToAvoidPlaying.condition: Wrist growth plate injury - can cause permanent damage if ignored
Spondylolysis flare-up - requires physiotherapy and rest

hydrationImportance.beforeMatch: 500 ml water 2 hours before; light snack if needed
hydrationImportance.afterMatch: 500 ml water or electrolyte drink; full meal within 1-2 hours
hydrationImportance.warningAvoid: Heavy meals before competition cause nausea during inversions and rotation skills
hydrationImportance.signsOfDehydration: Muscle cramps, dizziness, nausea, reduced focus

nutritionTips.preGame: Light complex carbs 2-3 hours before: oatmeal, toast with nut butter
nutritionTips.duringGame: Banana or energy gel between events
nutritionTips.postGame: Protein and carbs within 30 min; chocolate milk is ideal recovery drink
nutritionTips.daily: Adequate calories to support training load; do not under-fuel (RED-S risk)

physicalBurnout.limit: Max 25-30 hours training per week for elite; 10-15 for club
physicalBurnout.restDays: 1 full rest day minimum; 1-2 active recovery days
physicalBurnout.crossTrain: Swimming, dance, or Pilates for active recovery

mentalBurnout.signs: Fear of skills previously mastered, perfectionism causing anxiety, dreading gym
mentalBurnout.solution: Mental performance coach, reduce competitive pressure, rebuild confidence on easier skills

equipment.item: Gymnastics leotard or shorts and vest
Spring floor, vault, parallel bars, uneven bars, beam, rings, pommel horse

safetyGear.item: Spotting belt (for aerial skills in training)
Wrist braces (for gymnasts with wrist issues)

coordination.desc: Full-body kinesthetic awareness - knowing your body position in space
coordination.drill: Handstand holds (30-60 sec against wall); improve proprioception

speedAgility.desc: Explosive power for vault and tumbling run-ups

focusReaction.desc: Mental focus during high-pressure routines with no room for error

strategy.offensive: Maximise D score with high-difficulty connections
strategy.defensive: Prioritise clean execution over risky high-D skills in finals

xFactors.gameSense: Know when to attempt a risky skill vs play it safe
xFactors.decisionMaking: Choose routine composition that maximises total score

drillsForBeginners.drill: Start with side roll, then hurdle cartwheel
Back bend bridge hold for 10-30 seconds (spine flexibility)
Lie on back, press lower back down, arms overhead: 30 sec

focusingTechniques.technique: Deep breath, chalk hands, visualise routine, step up
Think only about the current skill, not the whole routine

pressureHandling.tip: If you fall, compose yourself in 5 seconds and continue confidently
Compete your best practice routine, not a perfect routine

sportsmanship.rule: Applaud competitors' good routines
Respect judges' scores and appeal through official channels only

howToJoinSport.step: Find a local gymnastics club with qualified coaching staff
Start in a recreational or beginner group class
Progress through graded levels (club, regional, national)
Register with national federation (e.g., USA Gymnastics, British Gymnastics)

qualitiesOfGoodPlayer.quality: Mental courage to attempt difficult skills
Artistic expression in floor and beam

strategies.strategy: Pack in difficult connections to maximise start value
Lower difficulty but perfect form to minimise deductions
One skill grade below training maximum for consistency

terminologies.term: D score - difficulty score based on skills performed
E score - execution score out of 10.0
Stuck landing - landing with no movement of feet
Salto - aerial somersault
Twisting - rotation around vertical axis in air
Roundoff - entry skill for tumbling (like cartwheel but feet together)
Back handspring - explosive back flip with hands
Yurchenko - vault with roundoff onto springboard, back handspring onto table
FIG - Federation Internationale de Gymnastique
Apparatus - the equipment used (beam, bars, vault, floor)

dimensions.contestArea: 8 m x 8 m (IJF) to 10 m x 10 m

courtFieldImage.surfaceTypes: Tatami mats (spring-loaded foam or compressed straw covered with rush)

healthBenefits.benefit: Builds full-body strength, especially grip and core
Teaches safe falling (ukemi) - reduces injury risk in daily life
Develops discipline, respect, and self-confidence
Excellent cardiovascular workout
Improves flexibility and balance

warmup.stretching: Neck rotations, wrist circles, hip flexor stretch, ukemi (breakfalls) practice (10 min)
warmup.pregameExercises: Uchikomi (throwing technique repetitions) with partner - 5 min

cooldown.postgameRecovery: Light partner stretching, shoulder and neck release, ice on any impact points

basicRules.rule: Contest area is the mat (tatami); stepping outside is penalised.
Win by ippon (full point): clean throw onto back, 20-second pin, submission, or opponent's disqualification.
Waza-ari (half point): near-ippon throw or 10-second pin; two waza-ari = ippon.
Shido (penalty) awarded for passivity, false attacks, or rule violations; 3 shido = disqualification.
Golden score: sudden death overtime if tied after regulation.

matchTimings.goldenScore: Unlimited until ippon or penalty decides

breaksIntervals.standard: Matte (stop) called by referee - brief pause to reset

scoringSystem.win: Ippon = immediate win; Waza-ari x2 = win; Shido x3 = opponent wins

rulesRegulations.howToWin: Score ippon, accumulate waza-ari, or opponent receives 3 shido penalties

foulsAndPenalties.penalty: Shido: warning for passivity or minor violation
Hansoku-make: disqualification for dangerous technique or third shido

commonInjuries.injury: Finger and thumb sprains from grip fighting
Knee ligament injury from leg sweeps
Neck strain from pins and strangles

preventionMethods.method: Master ukemi (safe falling technique) before learning throws
Tap out immediately when caught in a strangle or armlock
Tape fingers to prevent sprains during grip fighting
Wear ear guards to prevent cauliflower ear
Warm up neck thoroughly before sparring

diseasesToAvoidPlaying.condition: Neck injury - throws and pins can worsen cervical problems
Concussion - contact sport, head must be fully recovered

hydrationImportance.beforeMatch: 500 ml water 2 hours before; avoid heavy meals
hydrationImportance.duringMatch: Drink between matches at tournaments; 200 ml per break
hydrationImportance.afterMatch: 500-750 ml electrolyte drink to replace sweat lost through gi
hydrationImportance.warningAvoid: Never use dehydration for weight cutting - dangerous and impairs performance
hydrationImportance.signsOfDehydration: Muscle cramps, reduced power, dizziness, poor grip strength

nutritionTips.preGame: Rice, pasta or bread with lean protein 2-3 hours before
nutritionTips.duringGame: Banana or energy gel between matches at tournaments
nutritionTips.postGame: High protein meal within 1 hour; chocolate milk for recovery
nutritionTips.daily: High protein (1.6-2 g/kg) for muscle repair; complex carbs for energy

physicalBurnout.crossTrain: Strength and conditioning, swimming for active recovery

mentalBurnout.signs: Reluctance to randori, persistent soreness, loss of motivation
mentalBurnout.solution: Focus on technique over winning in sparring; take a week of light drilling only

equipment.item: Judogi (judo uniform) - IJF approved white or blue gi
Belt indicating rank (from white to black)
Ear guards (optional, recommended for heavy randori)

safetyGear.item: Knee brace for players with knee history

coordination.desc: Kuzushi (breaking opponent's balance) then timing the throw
coordination.drill: Uchikomi: repeat entry for one throw 50 times per session

speedAgility.desc: Explosive hip drive and footwork for throwing techniques

focusReaction.desc: React to opponent's movement and weight shift to initiate throws

strategy.offensive: Combination attacks: failed throw sets up second technique
strategy.defensive: Grip fighting to deny opponent their preferred grip

xFactors.gameSense: Use clock and shido count to decide risk level in golden score
xFactors.decisionMaking: Know when to go for ippon vs secure waza-ari lead

dailyPlan.day: Ukemi + uchikomi (50 reps x 3 throws) + light randori
Situation training (e.g., start from grip fight, from guard)

drillsForBeginners.drill: Mae ukemi (forward roll), yoko ukemi (side fall) - 20 reps each
Hip throw entry uchikomi without completing throw - 30 reps
Establish proper sleeve and lapel grip, maintain for 30 seconds against resisting partner

focusingTechniques.technique: Clear mind, react instinctively without over-thinking
Use the rei (bow) as a mental reset and focus signal

pressureHandling.tip: In golden score, focus on grip control not winning - points follow good grip
Breathe slowly between matte calls to stay calm and conserve energy

sportsmanship.rule: Always bow to partner before and after every practice
Tap immediately when caught - ego causes injuries
Respect all belt levels; a white belt deserves the same courtesy as a black belt

howToJoinSport.step: Find an IJF-affiliated dojo in your area
Purchase a basic judogi (white gi, $40-80)
Attend beginner group classes to learn ukemi and basic throws
Grade through kyu grades (coloured belts) toward black belt
howToJoinSport.costEstimate: Gi $40-120; club membership $50-150/year; competition entry $20-50; grading fees $15-40

qualitiesOfGoodPlayer.quality: Strong grip and upper body strength

strategies.strategy: Win the grip fight to dictate the match
Set up seoi-nage with ko-uchi-gari leg attack
After an unsuccessful throw, transition immediately to ground work
Use shido strategy in golden score - let opponent risk and penalise

terminologies.term: Ippon - full point, immediate win
Waza-ari - half point
Shido - penalty warning
Randori - free practice/sparring
Uchikomi - technique entry repetitions
Newaza - ground work (pins, chokes, armlocks)
Kuzushi - breaking opponent's balance
Ukemi - safe falling technique
Matte - referee call to stop
Hajime - referee call to begin
Judogi - judo uniform
Dojo - training hall
Sensei - instructor/teacher
IJF - International Judo Federation

courtFieldImage.surfaceTypes: Natural grass, artificial turf (FIFA certified), hybrid grass
courtFieldImage.venues: Stadiums, school pitches, public parks, indoor 5-a-side courts

healthBenefits.benefit: Burns 600-900 calories per match
Builds cardiovascular endurance (players run 10-13 km per match)
Develops leg strength, agility, and balance
Improves teamwork, communication, and leadership
Reduces risk of cardiovascular disease

warmup.stretching: Dynamic stretches: leg swings, high knees, butt kicks, hip circles (5-8 min)
warmup.pregameExercises: Light jog, passing drills, shooting practice (10 min)

cooldown.postgameRecovery: 5-minute jog, static stretching (quads, hamstrings, calves, hip flexors), foam rolling

basicRules.rule: 11 players per team; 3 substitutes allowed (up to 5 in some competitions).
Ball is in play until it fully crosses touchline or goal line.
No handling the ball (except goalkeeper in own penalty area).
Offside: attacker nearer to goal than second-last defender when ball is played.
Goal kick, corner kick, or throw-in depending on which team caused ball to go out.

matchTimings.extraTime: 2 x 15 minutes (if tied in knockout matches)
matchTimings.penalties: 5 kicks each then sudden death if still tied

breaksIntervals.standard: 15-minute halftime; water breaks in extreme heat

scoringSystem.win: 3 points in league; advance in knockout; goal = 1 point

rulesRegulations.howToWin: Score more goals than the opponent in 90 minutes

foulsAndPenalties.penalty: Foul: free kick; in penalty area: penalty kick from 12 yards
Yellow card: official warning (2 yellows = red)
Red card: immediate dismissal, team plays with 10 men

preventionMethods.method: Follow FIFA 11+ warm-up programme (proven to reduce injuries by 50%)
Wear shin guards in all matches and training
Strengthen hamstrings with Nordic curls
Use proper footwear for surface type (FG/SG/AG boots)
Land with bent knees when jumping for headers

diseasesToAvoidPlaying.condition: Hamstring Grade 2+ tear - at least 4-6 weeks rest required
Concussion - head injury protocol must be completed

hydrationImportance.beforeMatch: 500 ml water 2 hours before; 250 ml 15 min before kickoff
hydrationImportance.duringMatch: 150-200 ml at every water break and halftime
hydrationImportance.afterMatch: 750 ml water or electrolyte drink; continue hydrating for 2-3 hours
hydrationImportance.warningAvoid: Carbonated drinks before match; alcohol within 24 hours of game
hydrationImportance.signsOfDehydration: Reduced sprint speed, cramps, dizziness, poor decision-making

nutritionTips.preGame: Pasta or rice with lean protein 3 hours before; banana 1 hour before
nutritionTips.duringGame: Isotonic sports drink at halftime; orange slices for quick sugars
nutritionTips.postGame: Recovery protein + carbs within 30 min; full balanced meal within 2 hours

physicalBurnout.limit: 1-2 matches per week maximum; monitor weekly training load
physicalBurnout.restDays: 1-2 full rest days; active recovery on light days
physicalBurnout.crossTrain: Swimming or cycling on rest days for cardiovascular maintenance without impact

mentalBurnout.signs: Loss of motivation, poor on-field communication, persistent fatigue
mentalBurnout.solution: Vary training drills, set fresh goals, engage in team-building activities

equipment.item: Football (size 5 adult / size 4 youth / size 3 mini)
Football boots (FG for grass, AG for artificial, TF for astro)
Shin guards (mandatory in all organised matches)
Kit: jersey, shorts, socks (above shin guards)

coordination.desc: First touch control, passing accuracy, and shooting technique
coordination.drill: Rondo (passing circle): maintain possession in small space

speedAgility.desc: Explosive sprint speed and change of direction (COD)

focusReaction.desc: Read the game - anticipate where the ball will go before it arrives

strategy.offensive: Overlapping runs, through balls, pressing high to win possession
strategy.defensive: Compact block, press triggers, cover shadow

xFactors.gameSense: Know when to shoot, when to pass, when to dribble
xFactors.decisionMaking: Two-touch maximum in tight spaces; look before receiving the ball

dailyPlan.day: Technical skills - passing, dribbling, shooting (1.5 hr)
Tactical training - shape, set pieces (1.5 hr)

drillsForBeginners.drill: Pass against wall with inside of foot, control return - 5 min each foot
Dribble through 10 cones in zigzag with both feet
Shoot at small targets in corners of goal from 15 m

focusingTechniques.technique: Same music, same warm-up, same mental cues every match day
Think about your role and tasks, not the score

pressureHandling.tip: After making a mistake, take one deep breath and move on immediately
In penalty shootout, focus only on your technique, not the goalkeeper

sportsmanship.rule: Return ball to opposition after injury stoppage
Don't dive or simulate injury
Respect the referee's decisions

howToJoinSport.step: Find a local grassroots football club or school team
Buy basic boots and a ball ($30-80 starter budget)
Attend open training sessions or trials
Register with local football association for league play

strategies.strategy: Press opponents in their own half to win ball high up the pitch
Defend deep, win ball, attack quickly in transition
Short passing and movement to maintain possession
Ultra-defensive, protect a lead with 10+ men behind the ball

terminologies.term: Offside - attacker in offside position when ball is played
Throw-in - ball returned to play from touchline
Corner kick - awarded when defender puts ball over own goal line
Free kick - direct or indirect kick after foul
Penalty - awarded for foul in own penalty area
Dribbling - running with ball under control
Nutmeg - playing ball through opponent's legs
Hat-trick - 3 goals in one match by same player
Clean sheet - goalkeeper concedes no goals in match
FIFA - Federation Internationale de Football Association
VAR - Video Assistant Referee

courtFieldImage.surfaceTypes: Natural grass outfield; clay or loam pitch; can be mat pitch for lower levels
courtFieldImage.venues: Cricket grounds, stadiums, club grounds, park pitches

healthBenefits.benefit: Builds hand-eye coordination from batting and catching
Develops explosive power for bowling and batting
Improves concentration and tactical thinking over long formats
Aerobic exercise from fielding and running between wickets
Develops teamwork and strategic thinking

warmup.stretching: Shoulder rotations, hip flexor stretch, wrist rolls, spine rotations (10 min)
warmup.pregameExercises: Catching practice, batting throw-downs, short run fielding drills (15 min)

cooldown.postgameRecovery: Static stretches for shoulders, back, and hamstrings; ice on bowler's shoulder or knee

basicRules.rule: 11 players per side; batting team sends two batters to the crease.
Bowling team aims to dismiss (get out) all 10 batters.
Ways out: bowled, caught, LBW (leg before wicket), run out, stumped, hit wicket, handled ball.
Batters score runs by hitting the ball and running between wickets or hitting boundaries (4 = runs to boundary, 6 = over boundary).
Bowling: bowler delivers 6 legal balls = 1 over; same bowler cannot bowl consecutive overs.

matchTimings.test: Up to 5 days (2 innings per team)

breaksIntervals.standard: Lunch (40 min) and tea (20 min) breaks in Test cricket; drinks every 10 overs in ODI/T20

scoringSystem.win: Team with most runs wins; in Tests, win by runs or wickets or draw

rulesRegulations.howToWin: Score more runs than the opposition (or bowl them out for fewer)

foulsAndPenalties.penalty: No-ball: extra delivery bowled; batter cannot be out (except run out)
Wide: extra run added to batting team's total
Ball tampering: 5-run penalty and possible suspension

commonInjuries.injury: Stress fractures in back (especially junior fast bowlers)

preventionMethods.method: Limit overs bowled per session for junior cricketers (ICC guidelines)
Strengthen core and side muscles to protect spine
Wear helmet and body padding when batting against fast bowling
Practice catching with soft balls for beginners
Stretch shoulder and thoracic spine regularly

diseasesToAvoidPlaying.condition: Side strain - needs full healing before bowling resumes
Heat exhaustion - common in long outdoor formats; must rest and rehydrate

hydrationImportance.beforeMatch: 750 ml water from morning of match day; no large amounts just before
hydrationImportance.duringMatch: 200 ml every drinks break (every 10 overs); more in heat
hydrationImportance.afterMatch: 1 litre water or electrolyte drink after day's play
hydrationImportance.warningAvoid: Dehydration is serious in long formats - players can be on field for 6+ hours
hydrationImportance.signsOfDehydration: Cramps, fatigue, headache, reduced concentration at crease

nutritionTips.preGame: High-carb breakfast: porridge, toast, eggs on match morning
nutritionTips.duringGame: Lunch: balanced meal (rice/pasta + protein + salad); snacks between overs
nutritionTips.postGame: Recovery meal: protein and carbs within 30 min; full meal in evening
nutritionTips.daily: High protein for muscle maintenance; iron-rich foods to combat fatigue

physicalBurnout.limit: Fast bowlers: max 20-25 overs per day; rotate bowlers
physicalBurnout.restDays: 1-2 full rest days per week during season
physicalBurnout.crossTrain: Gym for strength; swimming for active recovery

mentalBurnout.signs: Dreading matches, poor concentration at crease, loss of enjoyment
mentalBurnout.solution: Play a shorter format for variety; take a break between series

equipment.item: Cricket bat (willow blade; size 6 youth to full adult)
Cricket ball (red leather for Test; white for ODI/T20)
Helmet with face grille (mandatory for batting against hard ball)
Cricket whites (Test) or coloured kit (limited overs)

safetyGear.item: Helmet (mandatory for all batting and wicketkeeping up to the stumps)
Chest guard and arm guard (optional for facing fast bowling)

coordination.desc: Hand-eye coordination for batting (picking line and length of delivery)
coordination.drill: Throw-down practice: coach feeds balls to specific areas for batter to hit

speedAgility.desc: Running between wickets, fielding sprints, bowler run-up speed

focusReaction.desc: Batters must react to 140+ km/h deliveries in under 0.5 seconds

strategy.offensive: Attacking batting: charge the bowler, use the sweep and slog sweep
strategy.defensive: Defensive batting: occupation of crease, play late, leave deliveries outside off-stump

xFactors.gameSense: Calculate run rate vs wickets in hand in limited overs
xFactors.decisionMaking: Field placement strategy based on batter's weaknesses

drillsForBeginners.drill: Partner tosses ball gently from side; batter hits to target
Simple catches at various heights to build confidence
Practice run-up and delivery action without ball first

focusingTechniques.technique: Focus your eyes on the ball from the bowler's hand; single cue word "ball"
Same pad-up routine, same walk to crease, same guard every time

pressureHandling.tip: After being dismissed cheaply, do a brief review then reset - the next innings starts fresh
In a run chase, focus on the current over not the total required

sportsmanship.rule: Walk when you know you're out (even if umpire doesn't give it)
Applaud opponent's good deliveries and catches
Spirit of cricket: play hard but play fair

howToJoinSport.step: Find a local cricket club and attend beginner coaching sessions
Buy basic equipment: bat and pads ($50-150 starter set)
Join a club age group or adult beginner team
Register with the national cricket board (e.g., ECB, BCCI, CA)
howToJoinSport.costEstimate: Starter bat and pads $50-150; helmet $30-80; club membership $50-200/year

qualitiesOfGoodPlayer.quality: Patience and concentration (especially in Test format)
Technical skill in batting or bowling

strategies.strategy: Yorkers and slower balls in final overs of T20 to restrict runs
Attack in first 6 overs when only 2 fielders outside circle
Move ball in air early with new ball to take top-order wickets
Unconventional shot to disrupt field placement

terminologies.term: Wicket - three stumps and two bails; also means dismissal of batter
Over - 6 legal deliveries bowled by same bowler
LBW - leg before wicket dismissal
No-ball - illegal delivery
Wide - delivery too wide for batter to play
Boundary - 4 runs (ball reaches boundary) or 6 (over boundary)
Duck - batter dismissed for 0 runs
Hat-trick - 3 wickets in 3 consecutive balls
Maiden over - over with no runs scored
DRS - Decision Review System (use of technology for umpire decisions)
ICC - International Cricket Council
Test cricket - 5-day format, highest form of the game
T20 - Twenty20 format, 20 overs per side

dimensions.fieldArea: Infield for jumps, throws, and pole vault

courtFieldImage.surfaceTypes: Synthetic rubber track (polyurethane); grass or dirt for cross country
courtFieldImage.venues: Athletics stadiums, school tracks, roads (marathon), cross country courses

healthBenefits.benefit: Outstanding cardiovascular fitness development
Burns 500-1000+ calories per session depending on event
Builds lean muscle mass (sprinting and throwing events)
Improves bone density especially in running and jumping events
Develops mental resilience and goal-setting habits

warmup.stretching: Dynamic: A-skips, B-skips, high knees, leg swings, arm swings (10 min)
warmup.pregameExercises: Strides (4 x 80 m at 90% effort), event-specific activation (blocks for sprinters)

cooldown.postgameRecovery: 10-min easy jog, full static stretch routine, foam rolling

basicRules.rule: Sprints: run in assigned lane; DQ for false start (one false start rule).
Relays: baton must be passed within 20 m exchange zone.
Long/Triple jump: take-off must be behind the board; foot over = no jump.
Throws: implement must land within sector; thrower must not leave circle before implement lands.
High jump/Pole vault: bar must not fall off for jump to count; 3 failures = eliminated.

breaksIntervals.standard: Heats, semis, finals across a 1-3 day competition schedule

scoringSystem.win: Fastest time or furthest/highest distance wins; points in multi-events (decathlon, heptathlon)

rulesRegulations.howToWin: Cross finish line first (track) or achieve the furthest/highest mark (field)

foulsAndPenalties.penalty: False start: DQ from race (one allowed in some junior formats)
Lane infringement: DQ if causing interference
No jump/throw: recorded as foul; 3 fouls = eliminated in field events

commonInjuries.injury: Stress fractures in tibia or foot

preventionMethods.method: Gradual mileage increase (10% rule per week)
Strengthen hamstrings with Nordic curls
Run on softer surfaces where possible
Replace running shoes every 500-800 km
Never skip warm-up before speed sessions

diseasesToAvoidPlaying.condition: Achilles tendinopathy flare - rest and physiotherapy required
Stress fracture - complete rest for 6-12 weeks

hydrationImportance.beforeMatch: 500 ml water 2 hours before; 250 ml 30 min before
hydrationImportance.duringMatch: Water at aid stations (marathon); sip between rounds (track)
hydrationImportance.afterMatch: 750 ml water or electrolyte drink immediately after
hydrationImportance.warningAvoid: Over-hydration (hyponatremia) risk in marathon - don't drink excessive plain water

nutritionTips.preGame: High-carb meal 3 hours before; sports gel or banana 30 min before for sprinters
nutritionTips.duringGame: Energy gels every 45 min in marathon (with water); nothing needed for short sprints
nutritionTips.postGame: Protein + carb recovery within 30 min; replenish glycogen stores
nutritionTips.daily: 5-7 g carbs/kg body weight for distance runners; 4-5 g/kg for sprinters

physicalBurnout.limit: Weekly mileage caps based on event; periodised training with off-season
physicalBurnout.restDays: 1-2 full rest days; base training vs peak training cycles
physicalBurnout.crossTrain: Cycling or swimming for aerobic maintenance without impact

mentalBurnout.signs: Dreading training, PBs stagnating despite effort, chronic fatigue
mentalBurnout.solution: Reduce volume and intensity; change training environment; race for fun

equipment.item: Running spikes (sprint, middle, cross country, or long jump spikes)
Training shoes (neutral or stability depending on gait)
Implements: discus, shot put, javelin, hammer (throwing events)

safetyGear.item: Sun protection (sunscreen, hat) for outdoor events

coordination.desc: Sprint mechanics: drive phase, maximal velocity, relay baton exchange
coordination.drill: Baton exchange drill: 4 athletes practice split timing for 20 m zone

speedAgility.desc: Explosive power from blocks; stride frequency and length optimisation

focusReaction.desc: React to starter's gun in under 0.15 sec for elite sprinters

strategy.offensive: Negative split in 800 m-1500 m for last-lap surge
strategy.defensive: Run tangents (shortest path) in road and cross country races

xFactors.gameSense: Race awareness - know your competitors' strengths and when to make a move
xFactors.decisionMaking: Choose to lead or follow based on conditions and competitors

dailyPlan.day: Speed session: 6 x 60 m at 95% effort
Tempo run: 4 x 800 m at 80% effort

drillsForBeginners.drill: March forward driving knee up to hip height alternately - 20 m x 4
Against wall, drive knee up rapidly in place - 15 sec x 3
From standing start, build speed over 60 m - 6 reps

focusingTechniques.technique: Same stretch, same strides, same breathing before every race
Relax (on drive phase), tall (at maximal velocity), smooth (in distance)

pressureHandling.tip: If false started, reset completely - forget the error and focus on the next start
In championship finals, race your race plan regardless of competitors

sportsmanship.rule: Congratulate competitors after races
Help fallen competitors where safe to do so

howToJoinSport.step: Find a local athletics club (most have beginner groups)
Try different events to find your best event
Buy suitable running shoes first; spikes come later
Register with national federation (e.g., UK Athletics, USATF)
howToJoinSport.costEstimate: Running shoes $60-150; club $30-80/year; spikes $40-150; competition licence $15-30

strategies.strategy: Run second half of race faster than first
Set fast pace from gun to break opponents psychologically
Sit in pack and unleash sprint finish in last 200 m
Mid-race acceleration to break away from pack

terminologies.term: PB - personal best time or distance
Blocks - starting blocks used in sprint events
False start - leaving blocks before starter's gun
Baton - relay stick passed between runners
Foul - invalid attempt in field events
Decathlon - 10-event competition (men)
Heptathlon - 7-event competition (women)
DNF - did not finish
DQ - disqualified
PW - personal worst (informal)
World Athletics - global governing body (formerly IAAF)
Road race - running race on public roads

dimensions.netHeight: 0.914 m at centre / 1.07 m at posts

courtFieldImage.surfaceTypes: Grass (Wimbledon), clay (Roland Garros), hard court (US/Australian Open), carpet (indoor)
courtFieldImage.venues: Tennis clubs, public courts, indoor centres, Grand Slam stadiums

healthBenefits.benefit: Burns 400-600 calories per hour
Develops full-body coordination and agility
Improves cardiovascular endurance and muscular strength
Enhances mental alertness, strategy, and focus
Low injury rate when proper technique is used

warmup.stretching: Shoulder arm swings, hip flexors, wrist rotations, calf raises, lateral lunges (5-8 min)
warmup.pregameExercises: Easy groundstroke rally from service line, then baseline; serve practice (10 min)

cooldown.postgameRecovery: Easy mini-tennis (close to net), static stretches for shoulders, forearm, calves

basicRules.rule: Match format: best of 3 sets (best of 5 in Grand Slams for men).
Set: first to 6 games with 2 clear game advantage; tiebreak at 6-6 (first to 7 points by 2).
Game: won at 40-30 advantage, or deuce then 2 consecutive points.
Serve: 2 attempts per point; first serve fault = second serve; double fault = point lost.
Ball is in play if it lands on the line or inside the court boundary.

matchTimings.match: Best of 3: 1-2 hours; Grand Slam best of 5: up to 5+ hours

breaksIntervals.standard: 90-second break at change of ends; 2 min between sets; medical timeout if injured

scoringSystem.win: Win games to win sets; win sets to win match

rulesRegulations.howToWin: Win the majority of sets (best of 3 or 5)

foulsAndPenalties.penalty: Foot fault on serve: counts as a fault
Hindrance: deliberate distraction = point penalty
Code violation: time warning, then point penalty, then game penalty

preventionMethods.method: Use correct grip size to reduce elbow stress
Strengthen rotator cuff with resistance bands
Wear court-specific shoes (different for clay vs hard court)
Don't over-grip racket when nervous
Forearm and wrist exercises daily to prevent elbow injury

diseasesToAvoidPlaying.condition: Tennis elbow flare - rest and physio; avoid topspin shots until healed
Rotator cuff tear - requires MRI and specialist assessment

hydrationImportance.beforeMatch: 500 ml water 2 hours before; 250 ml 15 min before
hydrationImportance.afterMatch: 750 ml water or electrolyte drink within 30 min
hydrationImportance.warningAvoid: Avoid caffeinated drinks within 2 hours of match - can cause irregular heartbeat in heat
hydrationImportance.signsOfDehydration: Cramping (common in long matches), reduced first step, poor ball toss on serve

nutritionTips.preGame: Pasta or rice 3 hours before; banana and nut butter 1 hour before
nutritionTips.duringGame: Banana at changeovers in long matches; sports drink for matches over 1.5 hours
nutritionTips.postGame: Protein shake within 30 min; full balanced meal within 2 hours
nutritionTips.daily: Balanced diet; carbohydrate load day before important matches

physicalBurnout.limit: Max 5-6 sessions per week; mix heavy and light days
physicalBurnout.restDays: 1 full rest day; periodise season with off-season training blocks
physicalBurnout.crossTrain: Swimming, running, or gym work for fitness without racket stress on elbow

mentalBurnout.signs: Unforced errors rising, dreading match play, persistent frustration
mentalBurnout.solution: Play social or doubles; focus on technique not results; take a week off competitive play

equipment.item: Tennis racket (adult frame: 27 inches; junior: 21-26 inches; appropriate grip size)
Tennis balls (pressurised for hard/grass; pressureless for clay)
Court-specific shoes (clay: herringbone sole; hard: durable sole; grass: pimples)

safetyGear.item: Proper tennis shoes for court surface (most important safety item)
Elbow support if prone to tennis elbow
Sunhat and sunscreen for outdoor courts

coordination.desc: Hand-eye coordination to time contact point; split-step timing before shots
coordination.drill: Ball machine practice: set to varied location at 60% pace, focus on contact point

speedAgility.desc: Explosive first step and recovery speed back to centre

focusReaction.desc: Read opponent's body, racket angle, and ball spin in under 0.5 seconds

strategy.offensive: Serve and volley; inside-out forehand to opponent's backhand
strategy.defensive: High heavy topspin to backhand to push opponent deep and reset

xFactors.gameSense: Know when to go for winner vs play high-percentage ball
xFactors.decisionMaking: Attack short balls; defend deep balls

dailyPlan.day: Serve practice (100 serves) + return of serve drills
Volleys and net play + overhead practice

drillsForBeginners.drill: Rally from service boxes over low net - builds control
Hit 20 consecutive cross-court forehands without error
Practice ball toss alone 50 times - toss is foundation of serve

focusingTechniques.technique: Say "bounce" when ball bounces, "hit" at contact - keeps focus on ball
Strings, breathe, walk, plan - same every point

pressureHandling.tip: At break point, focus on first serve percentage not outcome
After losing a close set, tell yourself "new match" for the next set

sportsmanship.rule: Call opponent's shots honestly even if it costs you a point
Don't celebrate opponent's unforced errors

howToJoinSport.step: Find a local tennis club or public court
Book group beginner lessons (often cheapest way to start)
Buy a mid-range racket ($50-100) suitable for beginners
Join a club membership for court access and social play
howToJoinSport.costEstimate: Racket $50-200; balls $8-20/can; court hire $10-25/hr; club membership $50-300/year

qualitiesOfGoodPlayer.quality: Mental strength in tiebreaks and deuce games

strategies.strategy: Big serve, follow to net, put away volley
High-margin topspin groundstrokes to wear opponent down
Run around backhand to hit powerful forehand cross-court
Surprise short ball to bring aggressive baseliner to net

terminologies.term: Ace - serve not touched by receiver
Double fault - two consecutive failed serves; point lost
Deuce - game score tied at 40-40
Advantage - point won at deuce; one more needed to win game
Tiebreak - 7-point game to decide set at 6-6
Fault - serve landing out of service box
Let - serve clips net and lands in; re-served
Break of serve - winning game on opponent's serve
Volley - hitting ball before it bounces
Overhead / smash - powerful shot above head
ITF - International Tennis Federation
Grand Slam - Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, US Open

courtFieldImage.surfaceTypes: Grass range (outdoor), wooden floor range (indoor), foam backstop
courtFieldImage.venues: Archery clubs, sports halls (indoor), outdoor ranges, Olympic venues

healthBenefits.benefit: Develops upper body strength (shoulders, back, core)
Excellent for focus, concentration, and mindfulness
Improves posture through consistent alignment training
Suitable for all ages and abilities including wheelchair users
Mental calm and stress reduction through meditative repetition

warmup.stretching: Shoulder rolls, arm circles, pectoral stretch, wrist flexion (5-8 min)
warmup.pregameExercises: Draw and hold (no arrow) 10 reps; 6 arrows at close range (5 m) for feel

cooldown.postgameRecovery: Shoulder and chest static stretches; release tension with arm shakes; note improvement areas

basicRules.rule: Archers shoot in defined ends (groups of arrows): 3 arrows (indoor) or 6 arrows (outdoor).
Scoring zones: 10 (inner gold), 9 (outer gold), 8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 (white outer).
Arrow breaking the line scores the higher value.
All archers must be on the shooting line at the same time per end.
No archer may retrieve arrows until all have shot and signal is given.

matchTimings.olympic: Set system: best of 5 sets (3 arrows each) per match

breaksIntervals.standard: Break between distances; drink and snack permitted on range

scoringSystem.win: Highest cumulative score wins; Olympic set system uses 2 pts per set win

rulesRegulations.howToWin: Score the most cumulative points across all ends

foulsAndPenalties.penalty: Calling arrow on wrong target: 0 scored for that arrow
Shooting after time signal: arrow not scored (or worst arrow removed)
Encroaching on safety zones: warning then disqualification

commonInjuries.injury: Rotator cuff strain from repetitive drawing

preventionMethods.method: Use an arm guard to prevent string slap
Use finger tab or mechanical release to protect fingers
Build draw weight gradually - don't exceed what you can hold for 5 seconds
Strengthen rotator cuff and back muscles in gym
Use a bow stringer - never brace a recurve bow by hand

diseasesToAvoidPlaying.condition: Rotator cuff tear - drawing bow will worsen injury significantly
Severe arthritis in fingers - seek lighter draw weight or compound bow

hydrationImportance.beforeMatch: 250-500 ml water before session; more in outdoor heat
hydrationImportance.duringMatch: Sip regularly between ends; water bottle on range is allowed
hydrationImportance.warningAvoid: Don't ignore hydration in outdoor summer sessions - standing in sun is deceptive
hydrationImportance.signsOfDehydration: Loss of focus, trembling draw arm, poor consistency

nutritionTips.preGame: Light meal: oats, eggs, or toast 1-2 hours before (avoid heavy food affecting steadiness)
nutritionTips.duringGame: Light snack between ends if session is 3+ hours
nutritionTips.postGame: Normal balanced meal; protein to support shoulder muscle recovery
nutritionTips.daily: Anti-inflammatory diet (omega-3, turmeric) for shoulder and elbow joint health

physicalBurnout.limit: Max 120-150 arrows per session; no more than 5 sessions per week
physicalBurnout.restDays: 2 rest days minimum; never shoot through pain
physicalBurnout.crossTrain: Shoulder and back strengthening in gym; yoga for flexibility

mentalBurnout.signs: Loss of concentration, dreading range sessions, inconsistent form despite practice
mentalBurnout.solution: Shoot at short distances for fun, remove scoring pressure, try a different style

equipment.item: Recurve or compound bow (beginners: recurve recommended; compound for hunting/3D)
Arrows (matched to bow draw weight and archer's draw length)

safetyGear.item: Finger tab or glove - protects drawing fingers
Chest guard - prevents string catching clothing
Always maintain arrow point downrange - never point at people

coordination.desc: Consistent anchor point, draw, and release every shot
coordination.drill: Blank bale at 1-2 m: shoot with eyes closed to feel perfect release

speedAgility.desc: Timed rounds: shoot 3 arrows in 120 seconds (Olympic) with accuracy

focusReaction.desc: Meditative focus: block out distractions, focus on process not target

strategy.offensive: Score 10s early to apply psychological pressure on opponent
strategy.defensive: Steady scoring with 8s and 9s beats inconsistent 10s and 5s

xFactors.gameSense: Know your average score and execute it under pressure
xFactors.decisionMaking: Aim at gold but accept 9 as a good shot - chasing 10s causes misses

dailyPlan.day: Blank bale technique (30 arrows at 2 m) + shoulder exercises
Long distance practice (50 m or 70 m)
Olympic simulation (5 sets x 3 arrows under 40 sec)

drillsForBeginners.drill: Draw bow with eyes closed; check alignment before opening eyes
10 draws holding for 5 sec, checking anchor point each time
Draw through the clicker slowly 10 reps for expansion feel

focusingTechniques.technique: Same pre-shot routine every arrow: stance, nock, draw, aim, expand, release
Each arrow is a fresh start; forget the last shot completely

pressureHandling.tip: In shoot-offs, focus on your routine not your competitor's score
If trembling, slow your breath and reduce draw weight pressure by rotating elbow out

sportsmanship.rule: Never coach or advise a competitor during competition
Help retrieve arrows carefully to avoid bending other archers' arrows

howToJoinSport.step: Attend a "have a go" or beginners' archery session at a local club
Complete a beginner's course (typically 6-8 sessions)
Buy starter recurve equipment after completing course ($150-300)
Join the club and register with national federation (e.g., World Archery affiliate)
howToJoinSport.costEstimate: Beginner course $100-200; starter recurve set $150-300; club membership $50-120/year

qualitiesOfGoodPlayer.quality: Mental calm and consistency under pressure
Repeatable technical form shot after shot
Patience for the slow improvement curve of archery

strategies.strategy: High average score beats volatile score with occasional 10s
Shoot between gusts in outdoor; learn to read flag or grass movement
Win the first set of an Olympic match to apply psychological pressure

terminologies.term: End - a group of arrows shot before scoring (3 or 6 arrows)
Gold - innermost scoring zone (10 or X ring)
Draw length - distance archer draws bowstring (personalised to each archer)
Draw weight - resistance in pounds at full draw
Clicker - metal plate that falls when archer draws to correct length
Anchor point - consistent point where draw hand contacts face
Stabiliser - rod attached to bow to reduce vibration and improve balance
Nock - plastic groove at arrow end that clips onto bowstring
Fletching - feathers or vanes at arrow tail for stability in flight
World Archery - international governing body (formerly FITA)
